response:
To find the modulus of the complex number $z$ satisfying $iz = \sqrt{3} - i$, we apply the property that $|iz| = |i||z| = |z|$, which implies $|z|$ is simply the modulus of the right-hand side expression $\sqrt{3} - i$.

Calculating this modulus yields $\sqrt{(\sqrt{3})^2 + (-1)^2} = \sqrt{3 + 1} = \sqrt{4} = 2$.
